summary: Gruber systematically dismantles Walt Mossberg's 'Drawbacks' section in his iPad 2 review, arguing that Mossberg bent over backwards to appear balanced by manufacturing criticisms. He shows that Mossberg listed battery life exceeding Apple's own specs as a negative, cited the lack of Flash despite Android tablets not delivering it well either, and dinged the iPad 2 for lacking 4G when even the Xoom shipped without it. Gruber notes the disproportionate word count: a third of Mossberg's iPad 2 review is drawbacks, while his Xoom review devotes one paragraph to downsides. The core argument is that forced balance in tech reviewing produces dishonest criticism when one product is clearly superior.
key_points: Mossberg listed battery life as a drawback despite the iPad 2 exceeding Apple's stated 10-hour spec by nine minutes
The Flash and 4G criticisms compare the iPad 2 against an imaginary ideal tablet rather than actual shipping competitors
A third of Mossberg's iPad 2 review covers drawbacks, while his review of the inferior Xoom dedicates only 62 words to downsides
Gruber argues that false balance in tech journalism distorts reality when one product is clearly dominant
The competing tablets Mossberg favorably compares to the iPad either don't deliver on their promises (Xoom's 4G) or deliver poorly (Flash performance)
money_quotes: The whole thing is a crock, an example of trying to be fair/balanced/objective by bending over backwards to find negative things to say about the device.
I'm not sure I've ever seen a review where a product that exceeds the manufacturer's stated specs for battery life gets dinged for battery life.
In the grand competitive landscape of today's tablet market, this is a major drawback of the iPad 2?
What is Mossberg comparing the iPad to? An imaginary tablet, available today, that does have Flash Player and 4G networking, on which neither technology has an adverse effect on battery life?
Why not list the lack of a quad-core processor, instead of the iPad 2's actual dual-core one, as a drawback, too?
Stating the plain truth, that the iPad 2 has no serious competition as a mainstream consumer device, doesn't make you biased. It makes you accurate.
key_insight: Forced journalistic balance produces dishonest reviews when critics manufacture drawbacks to avoid appearing biased toward a clearly superior product.
